verdict: 2026-07-06 10:46:41 PDT — The sketch is close but not sound as written. I would not build it this way without changing the ShellCheck pass/fail policy and narrowing the gitleaks allowlist. I checked the requested local files and exact local commands; gitleaks config semantics aligns with the upstream docs at https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks#configuration.

findings.title: ShellCheck gate fails on accepted info findings
findings.claim: The sketch runs `shellcheck $(shfmt -f .)` while treating the two SC2016 findings in `dev-docs/marketing/marketplace-cover.sh` as info-level and non-gating. That command exits 1 against the current tree on those exact findings, so the proposed `shell-lint` job would be red and AC1/AC2 cannot pass as written.
findings.alternative: Either add targeted `SC2016` suppressions beside the intentional ImageMagick currency literals and keep default ShellCheck, or make the policy explicit with `shellcheck -S warning $(shfmt -f .)` if CI should gate only warning/error correctness issues.
findings.win: Removes a guaranteed CI failure while preserving an explicit shell standard; the job no longer depends on a false assumption about ShellCheck exit codes.

findings.title: Path-only gitleaks allowlist creates whole-file blind spots
findings.claim: The proposed global `[[allowlists]] paths = [...]` suppresses every gitleaks finding in each listed path, not just the three known synthetic hits. Two of those paths are active test files, so a future real token committed there would be invisible to the external `/dev-audit` scanner.
findings.alternative: Keep `[extend] useDefault = true`, but split the exceptions into narrow allowlists that combine `paths` with exact `regexes`, `stopwords`, `targetRules`, or `regexTarget = "line"` for the known synthetic fixture text. Use native `gitleaks:allow` line comments where they can cover current-file fixtures without relying on history-wide file exemptions.
findings.win: Still silences the known false positives, but keeps default gitleaks coverage active for the rest of each file; the invariant becomes 'these synthetic fixture strings are allowed' instead of 'these files are unscanned'.
